note: Exact SMMS name match (UMLS C0702166). TCM 痤疮 (Cuo Chuang) is the canonical TCM dermatology term for acne.

SMMS only carries 'Aging Premature' (a narrower clinical concept). Duke 'Aging' is a Duke bioactivity tag for general anti-aging activity, not a clinical diagnosis -- the premature-aging mapping is the closest analog. TCM 早衰 (Zao Shuai = early decline) is the analogous TCM concept.

SMMS has no allergy/hypersensitivity row (vocabulary skews toward post-operative and TCM-aligned modern symptoms). SMTS does not surface an allergy concept either. Honest unmatched -- bioactivity tag is too generic to map without losing fidelity.
Exact SMMS match (UMLS C0003467). TCM 焦虑 (Jiao Lv) is the modern Chinese clinical term for anxiety also used in TCM.
Exact SMMS match. TCM does not have a single-character 'arthritis' equivalent; 关节痹痛 (joint bi-syndrome pain) is the classical TCM analog used for inflammatory joint disease.

Reviewer upgrade: scaffolder picked 'Asthma Attack' (878, an acute exacerbation); 'Bronchial Asthma' (509) is the canonical SMMS term and matches Duke's general bioactivity. TCM 哮喘 is the standard term.

tcm_name_en: Bacterial infection (Xi Jun Xing Gan Ran)
note: Exact concept match (singular vs plural; same UMLS C0004623). TCM term is a direct modern-Chinese rendering.

SMMS lacks a precise 'bile insufficiency' / cholestasis row; biliary-gallbladder disorder is the closest broader category. TCM 肝胆病 (liver-gallbladder syndrome) is the classical analog covering bile-flow dysfunction.

Reviewer upgrade: scaffolder mis-picked 'Bloody Stool'. 'Blood Coagulation' (UMLS C0005778) is the exact concept Duke's herbs target. TCM side is awkward -- 出血 (bleeding) is the inverse symptom; herbs that promote clotting are studied 'against' bleeding. Tagged low -- agents should treat this as a process/mechanism tag, not a symptom.

SMMS has no top-level 'Cancer' row; 'Carcinomas' (UMLS C0007097) is the most generic neoplastic term. SMMS does carry specific cancers (cervical, colorectal, mammary) but Duke's bioactivity tag is generic. TCM 癌肿 (Ai Zhong = cancer-mass) is a generic TCM cancer term.

Reviewer upgrade: 'Cognitive Impairment' (UMLS C0338656) is the standard clinical synonym for cognitive decline -- upgraded to high. TCM 健忘 (forgetfulness) is the classical analog; 健忘 covers age-related cognitive decline in TCM.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 便秘 is the standard term.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 咳嗽 is the canonical term.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 抑郁 is the modern-Chinese clinical term used in both Western and TCM contexts.

Reviewer upgrade: 'Diabetes Mellitus' is the canonical clinical name for diabetes (UMLS C0011849); upgraded to high. TCM 消渴 (Xiao Ke = wasting-thirst) is the classical TCM term for diabetes mellitus -- documented in Huangdi Neijing as the closest TCM equivalent.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 腹泻 is the standard modern term; 泄泻 (Xie Xie, id 1318) is the classical-TCM variant.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 湿疹 (literally 'damp rash') is the standard term.
Exact SMMS match (UMLS C0015672). Verified NOT to match SMMS 379 'Fatigue Fracture' (orthopedic). TCM 乏力 is the canonical fatigue/lassitude term; correlates with qi-deficiency (气虚) syndrome.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 发热 is the canonical term for fever.

Reviewer upgrade: scaffolder marked unmatched (Duke uses 'fluid retention', SMMS uses 'Edema'). Edema (UMLS C0013604) is the medical synonym -- upgraded to high. TCM 水肿 (water swelling) is the canonical TCM term.

SMMS has no fungal/mycosis/candidiasis row in its 1148 entries. Scaffolder picked 'Skin Infection' (284) which is wrong (could be bacterial/viral). Honest unmatched -- agents should resolve fungal infection to specific organisms, not symptoms.
Reviewer fix: scaffolder mis-picked 'Glossitis' on a token-overlap fluke. Alopecia (UMLS C0002170) is the universal medical term for hair loss. TCM 脱发 is the standard term.

SMMS has no generic 'heart disease' row; 'Arteriosclerotic Heart Disease' (UMLS C0010054) is the most prevalent specific form and the closest analog. TCM 胸痹 (chest bi = chest impediment) is the classical term Zhang Zhongjing used in Jingui Yaolue for what we now call ischemic heart disease.

SMMS has no 'hyperglycemia' or 'high blood glucose' row. Diabetes Mellitus is the related diagnosis but conceptually distinct from the symptom of elevated blood sugar -- tagged low. TCM 消渴 covers polydipsia/polyuria/polyphagia which are downstream of hyperglycemia.
Reviewer upgrade: scaffolder picked 'Rebound Hypertension' (623, narrower). 'Essential Hypertension' (UMLS C0085580) is canonical primary hypertension. TCM 高血压 is the direct modern-Chinese term.

SMMS has no immunodeficiency / immune-dysfunction row in its 1148 entries (checked 'immune', 'immuno*'). Scaffolder mis-picked 'Sexual Dysfunction' on the 'dysfunction' token. Honest unmatched -- this Duke tag is mechanistic and would need DSL-side handling.
Exact SMMS match (UMLS C0021368). TCM 炎症 is the modern-Chinese term universally used.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 失眠 is the canonical term.

Reviewer fix: scaffolder picked 'Liver Pain' (260, wrong -- pain != damage). SMMS has no 'liver injury' / 'hepatotoxicity' row; 'Hepatobiliary Disease' (UMLS C0267792) is the broadest analog. TCM 肝硬化 (liver cirrhosis) is the canonical end-stage liver damage term in modern TCM.
SMMS has no immunodeficiency / immunocompromised / 'low immunity' row. Scaffolder mis-picked 'Decreased Urine Flow' on the 'low/decreased' token. Honest unmatched.

tcm_name_en: Decreased sexual desire (Xing Yu Jian Tui)
note: Reviewer upgrade: 'Lack Of Libido' is a near-exact synonym of 'Low libido' (both UMLS C2981158-aligned) -- upgraded to high. TCM 性欲减退 is the direct modern-Chinese clinical term; could also map to 阳痿 (Yang Wei) for male impotence specifically but Duke's tag is broader.

Reviewer fix: scaffolder mis-picked 'Decreased Urine Flow' on the 'low/decreased' token. 'Lactation Decreased' (UMLS C0020610) is the exact clinical synonym. TCM 乳少 is the canonical TCM term for hypogalactia.
Reviewer upgrade: 'Memory Disorder' (UMLS C0025261) is the canonical SMMS row for memory decline -- upgraded to high. TCM 记忆减退 is the direct clinical translation.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 偏头痛 (one-sided headache) is the canonical term and a direct etymological cognate.

Reviewer upgrade: scaffolder picked 'Masseter Muscle Spasm' (203, jaw-specific). 'Spasm' (UMLS C0037763) is the generic SMMS row. TCM 抽搐 covers convulsions/spasms broadly; tagged medium because Duke's 'muscle spasm' is more specifically peripheral whereas 抽搐 leans neurological.
Exact SMMS match. TCM 恶心 is the canonical modern-Chinese clinical term.

SMMS has no neurodegeneration / Alzheimer / Parkinson / dementia rows in its 1148 entries (vocabulary skews toward symptoms not pathophysiology). Honest unmatched -- agents should resolve through Duke's compound-target links to neurological diseases instead.

tcm_name_en: Obese body shape (Xing Ti Fei Pang)
note: Exact SMMS match. TCM 形体肥胖 (literally 'body-form fat') is the canonical TCM term for obesity.

Oxidative stress is a biochemical mechanism, not a clinical symptom. SMMS (a clinical-symptom vocabulary) has no analog. Scaffolder mis-picked 'Emotional Distress' on the 'stress' token. Honest unmatched -- agents should resolve via Duke compound-bioactivity tags (antioxidant) rather than symptom mapping.

Exact SMMS match (UMLS C0030193). SMTS has 200+ pain-flavored rows (背痛, 头痛, 胃痛, etc.) but no single-character 痛 (Tong) row at the generic level -- TCM treats pain by location/etiology, never as a free-floating concept. Mark TCM as not_applicable rather than picking a misleading specific.

SMMS has only narrow specific rows (Hookworm Infections, Filariasis, Parasitemia) -- no generic 'parasitic infection' concept. Scaffolder mis-picked 'Skin Infection' (284). Honest unmatched -- agents should resolve to specific organisms downstream.

tcm_name_en: Blood-stasis blocking meridians (Yu Xie Zu Luo)
note: SMMS has no peripheral-vascular-disease / poor-circulation row. Scaffolder mis-picked 'Poor Quality Sleep' on the 'poor' token. TCM 瘀血阻络 (blood stasis blocking meridians) IS the classical TCM analog for circulatory stagnation -- one of the rare cases where TCM offers a better mapping than modern medicine for a Duke bioactivity tag.

Reviewer fix: scaffolder mis-picked 'Dry Skin' on the 'skin' token. Dermatitis (UMLS C0011603) is the clinical synonym for skin inflammation. TCM 红肿 (redness-and-swelling) is the classical descriptive sign for cutaneous inflammation.

SMMS has no 'gastric ulcer' or 'peptic ulcer' as a generic row -- only 'Gastric Ulcer Bleeding' (911, complication-specific). Generic 'Ulcer' (695) is the closest analog. TCM 胃痛 (stomach pain) is the classical syndrome under which gastric ulcer presents in TCM.

tcm_name_en: Emotional depression / liver-qi stagnation (Qing Zhi Yi Yu)
note: Reviewer upgrade: 'Emotional Distress' (UMLS C0700361) is the closest SMMS analog for psychological stress -- upgraded to high. TCM 情志抑郁 (emotional-depression / liver-qi stagnation) is the canonical TCM stress concept.

SMMS has only narrow specific rows (Virus 280, Animal Virus 281, Arbovirus 456, Viral Hepatitis 1140) -- no generic 'viral infection' concept. Scaffolder mis-picked 'Skin Infection' (284). Honest unmatched -- agents should resolve to specific viruses downstream.

Wound healing is a therapeutic process (the goal of treatment), not a presenting symptom. SMMS rows 'Wound Infection' and 'Injuries Wounds' describe injuries, not healing -- mapping would invert the polarity. Honest unmatched on the modern side. TCM 疮疡 (sores-and-ulcers) is the broad classical category under which wound-healing herbs are indicated.
